title: The Turn of the Screw
author: James, Henry
summary: The Turn of the Screw recounts a governess’s unsettling tenure at Bly, an isolated country estate where she cares for two young wards, Flora and Miles. Hired by their enigmatic guardian, she encounters hints of previous tragedy and reports of spectral figures. Mrs. Grose and the household are uneasy; the governess grows convinced that apparitions of a former valet and a previous governess haunt the children. She promises silence to the master while struggling with mounting dread, enigmatic signs, and the children’s odd behavior. The narrator frames the governess’s manuscript read by Douglas, who preserves and relays its chilling impressions.

characters.name: Narrator (I)
characters.personality: observant, framing storyteller; attentive and reflective
characters.lifespan: alive while recounting; presents Douglas's manuscript

characters.name: Douglas
characters.personality: cultivated, dramatic, secretive; enjoys relating the manuscript
characters.lifespan: alive when he read the manuscript but later dies; entrusted manuscript to narrator

characters.name: Mrs. Griffin
characters.personality: curious, gossipy member of the fireside company

characters.name: Griffin (Mr. Griffin)
characters.personality: participates wryly in the company’s banter

characters.name: The Governess (manuscript author)
characters.lifespan: author of the manuscript; in the framing account she is reported as dead (the pages were sent before she died)

characters.name: Mrs. Grose
characters.personality: practical, affectionate toward the children, loyal housekeeper
characters.lifespan: alive and acting as housekeeper/superintendent at Bly

characters.name: Miles
characters.personality: polished but mysteriously affected; central child-ward
characters.lifespan: at Bly during the governess’s account; fate not fully revealed in excerpt

characters.name: Flora
characters.personality: sweet, much looked after by Mrs. Grose; naive
characters.lifespan: at Bly during the governess’s account; fate not fully revealed in excerpt

characters.name: Former Governess (the deceased predecessor)
characters.age: older than her successor (text: "she was ten years older")
characters.lifespan: died (her death precipitated the new governess’s appointment)

characters.name: The Guardian / Bachelor (employer)
characters.personality: handsome, off-hand, kind, gallant but distant; imposes strict condition of non-interference
characters.lifespan: alive and guardian of the children; remains remote and enigmatic

characters.name: Narrator's Sister
characters.personality: mentioned indirectly as employer relation; not central
characters.lifespan: alive when referenced; her former governess was known to narrator

characters.name: Cook (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: serves at Bly during the governess’s tenure

characters.name: Housemaid (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: serves at Bly during the governess’s tenure

characters.name: Dairywoman (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: serves at Bly during the governess’s tenure

characters.name: Old Groom (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: serves at Bly during the governess’s tenure

characters.name: Old Gardener (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: serves at Bly during the governess’s tenure
